sections.content:
Now I see the checkered pattern clearly. It's composed of single-pixel blue (b) and charcoal (c) pieces at positions:
- [20,19], [20,21] - blue
- [21,18] - blue, [21,19] - charcoal, [21,20] - blue, [21,21] - charcoal
- [22,19] - blue, [22,20] - charcoal, [22,21] - blue
- [23,18] - blue, [23,19] - charcoal, [23,20] - blue, [23,21] - charcoal
This is a 4x4 checkered pattern (alternating blue and charcoal). It's located at [20,18]-[23,21], which is exactly where the blue square was initially!
So the blue square at [20,18]-[23,21] was transformed into a checkered pattern when I clicked on the blue target. The blue square then moved to [34,8]-[37,11].
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-examine.
Initial state:
- Blue square (b) at [20,18]-[23,21] (4x4 blue square)
Current state:
- Checkered pattern at [20,18]-[23,21] (4x4 checkered blue/charcoal)
- Blue square (b) at [34,8]-[37,11] (4x4 blue square)
So the blue square was at [20,18]-[23,21] initially, and after clicking the blue target:
1. The blue square at [20,18]-[23,21] was transformed into a checkered pattern
2. A new blue square appeared at [34,8]-[37,11]
Wait, that's weird. Let me check if the blue square at [34,8]-[37,11] is the same object as the original blue square, or if it's a new object.
Actually, looking at the initial state, the blue square was at [20,18]-[23,21]. After clicking the blue target, the blue square moved to [34,8]-[37,11], and the original position [20,18]-[23,21] became a checkered pattern.
So the game mechanic is:
1. Clicking a target collects the matching color from the left side
2. The collected color moves to a new position
3. The original position becomes a checkered pattern (indicating it was collected)
But wait, the red bar was at [30,12]-[33,17] initially, and after clicking the red target, it moved to [24,18]-[29,21]. The original position [30,12]-[33,17] is now empty (or part of the background).
Let me check if there's a checkered pattern at [30,12]-[33,17].
Actually, looking at the current state, the red bar is at [24,18]-[29,21], which is a 6x4 red bar (24 pixels). The original red bar was also at [30,12]-[33,17], which is a 4x4 red bar (16 pixels). Wait, that doesn't match.
Let me re-examine. The initial red bar was at [30,12]-[33,17], which is rows 30-33 (4 rows) and cols 12-17 (6 cols), so it's a 4x6 = 24 pixel red bar. The current red bar is at [24,18]-[29,21], which is rows 24-29 (6 rows) and cols 18-21 (4 cols), so it's a 6x4 = 24 pixel red bar.
So the red bar was rotated from 4x6 to 6x4 when it was collected. Interesting.
Similarly, the blue square was at [20,18]-[23,21], which is a 4x4 blue square (16 pixels). The current blue square is at [34,8]-[37,11], which is also a 4x4 blue square (16 pixels). So the blue square wasn't rotated, just moved.
OK so the game mechanic seems to be:
1. Clicking a target collects the matching color from the left side
2. The collected color moves to a new position (and possibly rotates)
3. The original position becomes a checkered pattern (for the blue square) or empty (for the red bar)
But I still don't understand what the goal is. I've collected both red and blue, and the level isn't complete.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the game is about matching the left side pattern to the right side pattern. The right side has red and blue fills, and the left side has colored objects that need to be arranged to match.
Or maybe the game is about collecting all colors, and there are more colors to collect. The left side still has:
- Yellow dot (Y)
- Dark red box (r)
- Gray boxes (g)
- Green dot (N)
Maybe I need to collect these colors too? But there are no corresponding targets on the right side.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the right side again. The right side has:
- Red fill at [17,42]-[21,54]
- Blue fill at [34,42]-[38,54]
These are the "collected" colors displayed on the right side. But maybe the game is about arranging these colors in a specific order or position.
Let me try clicking on the right-side fills to see if they do something. The red fill is at [17,42]-[21,54] and the blue fill is at [34,42]-[38,54].
Actually, I just realized something. The right side has white dots along the divider at cols 32-33. These might be indicators of which colors have been collected. Let me count them:
- [10,32]-[11,33]
- [14,32]-[15,33]
- [18,32]-[19,33]
- [22,32]-[23,33]
- [26,32]-[27,33]

- [34,32]-[35,33]
- [38,32]-[39,33]
- [42,32]-[43,33]
- [46,32]-[47,33]
- [50,32]-[51,33]
That's 11 white dots. Maybe each dot represents a color that needs to be collected, and the dots change color or disappear when the corresponding color is collected.
But I've only collected 2 colors (red and blue), and all 11 dots are still white. So maybe the dots don't represent collected colors.
Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the game is about moving the green dot to a specific position to trigger level completion. The green dot is currently at [38,12]-[39,13], which is at the top-right corner of the gray box.
Let me try moving the green dot to different positions and see if anything happens. The gray box is at [38,8]-[43,13], so the green dot can be at positions:
- Top-left: [38,8]-[39,9]
- Top-right: [38,12]-[39,13] (current position)
- Bottom-left: [42,8]-[43,9]
